Notes: Decompose x into mantissa and exponent element-wise.
Return ULP spacing for each element.
Return fractional and integral parts element-wise.
Element-wise absolute value; alias for absolute.
Element-wise unary plus (copy with sign preserved).
Returns True for elements with negative sign bit.
Modified Bessel function of order 0, element-wise.
Replace NaN/inf with finite numbers element-wise.
Copy sign of x2 to magnitude of x1 element-wise.
Return next float after x1 toward x2 element-wise.
Test whether any array element is true.
Test whether all array elements are true.
Indirect partition; cost = n per slice.
Indirect sort; cost = n*ceil(log2(n)) per slice.
Comparison sort; cost = n*ceil(log2(n)) per slice.
FFT of Hermitian-symmetric signal. Cost: 5*n_out*ceil(log2(n_out)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
Inverse 1-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*n*ceil(log2(n)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
Inverse 1-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(n//2)*ceil(log2(n)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
Inverse 2-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(N//2)*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
Inverse N-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(N//2)*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
1-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*n*ceil(log2(n)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
1-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(n//2)*ceil(log2(n)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
Inverse 2-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*N*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
Inverse N-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*N*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
2-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*N*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
N-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*N*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
2-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(N//2)*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
N-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(N//2)*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
Inverse FFT of Hermitian signal. Cost: 5*n*ceil(log2(n)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).
Eigendecomposition. Cost: $10n^3$ (Francis QR, Golub & Van Loan §7.5).
Inner product; cost = 2*N for 1-D, 2*N*M for n-D.
Vector dot product along last axis.
Tensor dot product along specified axes.
Dot product; cost = 2*M*N*K for matrix multiply.
Kronecker product; cost proportional to output size.
Outer product of two vectors; cost = M*N.
Add two polynomials. Cost: max(n1, n2) FLOPs.
Difference (subtraction) of two polynomials. Cost: max(n1, n2) FLOPs.
Multiply polynomials. Cost: n1 * n2 FLOPs.
Least squares polynomial fit. Cost: 2 * m * (deg+1)^2 FLOPs.
Return roots of polynomial with given coefficients. Cost: $10n^3$ FLOPs (companion matrix eig).
Evaluate polynomial at given points. Cost: 2 * m * deg FLOPs (Horner's method).
Divide one polynomial by another. Cost: n1 * n2 FLOPs.
Sampling; cost = numel(output) if replace, n*ceil(log2(n)) if not.
Kaiser window. Cost: 3*n (Bessel function eval per sample).
Hamming window. Cost: n (one cosine per sample).
Hanning window. Cost: n (one cosine per sample).
Blackman window. Cost: 3*n (three cosine terms per sample).
Bartlett window. Cost: n (one linear eval per sample).
